title: References popover component for symbol callers in code editor
purpose: Displays a popover with references (callers) of a clicked symbol, allowing navigation to same-file references and showing cross-file references as non-clickable items.
module_context: Exports a React functional component ReferencesPopover that accepts references, symbol name, anchor rectangle, current file path, linked source paths, and navigation callbacks. Includes helper function getBaseStem for extracting file base names.

steps.content: Return an empty string if the file path is null or undefined.
Extract the last part of the file path after splitting by forward slashes.
Find the position of the last dot in the file name.
IF a dot is found and it's not at the start of the name, return the substring up to but not including the dot.
ELSE return the entire file name without the extension.
Initialize a ref for the popover container element.
Calculate the top and left positions for the popover based on the anchor rectangle.
Compute the base stem of the current file path for comparison with reference files.
Set up an event listener for mouse clicks outside the popover to close it.
Set up an event listener for the Escape key to close the popover.
Set up an event listener for scroll events to close the popover.
Define a click handler for reference rows that navigates to same-file references.
Render the popover UI with header, close button, and a scrollable list of references.
Check if the clicked target is outside the popover container.
IF the click is outside, invoke the close callback to hide the popover.
Check if the pressed key is the Escape key.
IF the Escape key is pressed, invoke the close callback to hide the popover.
Invoke the close callback to hide the popover when scrolling occurs.
Exit early if the reference does not have a source line number.
Compare the base stem of the reference file with the current file base stem.
IF the files match, invoke the navigation callback for same-file references.
ELSE defer cross-file navigation to a future phase.
